title: MASA Membership Protections & Benefits

sections.heading: Emergency Ground Ambulance Transport Protection
sections.text: MASA provides payment for eligible out-of-pocket expenses incurred by ground ambulance transportation services resulting from a serious emergency.

sections.heading: Emergency Air Ambulance Transport Protection
sections.text: MASA provides payment for eligible out-of-pocket expenses incurred by air ambulance transportation resulting from a serious emergency. All flights must be ordered by a first responder or transferring physician who deems air transport medically necessary.

sections.heading: Hospital to Hospital Air Ambulance Transport Protection
sections.text: MASA provides payment for eligible out-of-pocket expenses for physician-ordered hospital-to-hospital air ambulance transport when a specialized level of care is not available at the member's current facility.

sections.heading: Hospital Private Room Upgrade
sections.text: MASA reimburses eligible out-of-pocket expenses for choosing a private hospital room after using emergency transportation.

sections.heading: Post-Admission Continued Care Transport Protection
sections.text: MASA reimburses eligible expenses up to $500 for non-emergency ground transportation or ride-sharing between a medical facility, rehabilitation facility, skilled nursing facility, long-term care facility, hospice facility, or home, to attain continued care resulting from an emergency.

sections.heading: Repatriation to Hospital Near Home Transport
sections.text: MASA helps coordinate and pay for air or ground transportation for a member to return home for continued care when hospitalized more than 100 miles away from home.

sections.heading: Minor Return Transport Protection
sections.text: MASA provides payment for the return of a minor (below age 18) to a parent, legal guardian, or responsible person if the minor is left unattended due to an ambulance transport.

sections.heading: Grandchildren and Great Grandchildren Protection
sections.text: MASA extends support for all Emergency Shield plan benefits (excluding air ambulance transport benefits) to a member's grandchildren and great-grandchildren up to age 14. For Lifetime memberships, this extends to grandchildren up to age 26.

sections.heading: Extended Hospital Stay Incidental Expense Protection
sections.text: MASA reimburses eligible incidental expenses (hotels, food, transportation, etc.) if a member is hospitalized over 100 miles from home for 7 days or more, limited to two reimbursements per consecutive 12-month period.

sections.heading: Patient Return Transport
sections.text: MASA helps coordinate and pay for a commercial flight back to a member's residence once discharged from the hospital following an emergency occurring more than 100 miles away from home.

sections.heading: Companion Emergency Transport Protection
sections.text: MASA reimburses eligible out-of-pocket expenses for a companion accompanying a member in an emergency transportation vehicle.

sections.heading: Companion Return Transport
sections.text: If a member is hospitalized more than 100 miles from home, MASA helps coordinate and pay for return transportation for a companion from the hospitalization location back to their home.

sections.heading: Hospital Visitor Ground Transport Protection
sections.text: For emergencies near home, MASA reimburses a visitor's parking and taxi/ride-share expenses up to $200 to visit a member during an inpatient stay, limited to two reimbursements per 12-month period.

sections.heading: Organ Recipient Transport
sections.text: MASA helps coordinate and pay for air transportation for a member to fly to the commercial airport nearest the medical facility for a scheduled organ transplant.

sections.heading: Hospital Visitor Air Transport
sections.text: MASA helps coordinate and pay for a round-trip ticket for a visitor of the member's choice when an emergency occurs more than 100 miles from home.

sections.heading: Vehicle and RV Return
sections.text: MASA helps coordinate and pay for the return of an owned or rental vehicle/RV to a member's residence (or rental company) if the member is taken from it due to emergency transport, including fuel, oil, and a qualified driver.

sections.heading: Pet Return Transport Protection
sections.text: MASA provides payment for the return of a pet left behind as a result of an ambulance transport.

sections.heading: Organ Retrieval Transport
sections.text: MASA reimburses eligible out-of-pocket expenses associated with transporting an organ to be retrieved and used for a member's organ transplant.

sections.heading: Mortal Remains Return Transport
sections.text: MASA provides informational support and reimburses eligible expenses to transport a member's remains back to their area of residence via air, ground, or ferry if the member passes away more than 100 miles from home.

sections.heading: Emergency Water Ambulance Transport Protection
sections.text: MASA reimburses eligible out-of-pocket expenses for water ambulance transportation staffed with emergency personnel after a serious emergency, limited to one reimbursement per 12-month period.

sections.heading: Treat and No Transport
sections.text: MASA reimburses eligible out-of-pocket expenses for emergency medical treatment by a summoned first responder even when no ambulance transport occurs, limited to two reimbursements per 12-month period.

sections.heading: Physician Search, Nurse Helpline, Attorney Search, Translation Services, Global Advisor, Emergency Message Relay, Prepay Foreign Currency, Concierge Service
sections.text: These benefits are exclusive to Lifetime membership plans. They include locating a licensed physician or attorney abroad, connecting members to a registered nurse for guidance, telephonic translation during emergencies, tailored destination health/safety guidance, relaying messages between a member and loved ones, prepaying up to $50,000 in foreign currency for emergency transport, and concierge coordination for trip disruptions.

sections.heading: Sick While Away From Home Expense Protection
sections.text: MASA reimburses eligible expenses for contracting a qualifying communicable disease or becoming too ill to return home while more than 100 miles away, including accommodation, meals, testing, medical facility visits, and airline re-booking, limited to two reimbursements per 12-month period.

sections.heading: Medical Payments Advisor
sections.text: MASA's advisors help verify policy enrollment, confirm medical benefits coverage, and provide guidance on medical expenses due for bills resulting from an inpatient medical stay.

sections.heading: Cruise Ship Evacuation and Return Transport Protection
sections.text: Exclusive to Emergency Shield Plus plans: MASA reimburses eligible out-of-pocket expenses for physician-ordered air ambulance evacuation off a cruise ship when specialized care isn't available on board, and helps coordinate one-way airfare for the member and a companion to rejoin the cruise or return home.

sections.heading: Worldwide (outside U.S.) Emergency Transport Protections
sections.text: Exclusive to Emergency Shield Plus plans: MASA reimburses eligible expenses for ground and air ambulance transport, hospital-to-hospital transport, water ambulance transport, vehicle/RV return, and transportation interruption expenses when a serious emergency occurs outside the United States.

sections.heading: Hospitalization Protection
sections.text: Exclusive to Emergency Shield Plus Lifetime membership plans: MASA provides $5,000 when a member is admitted for an inpatient medical stay resulting from a serious emergency while outside the United States, limited to two payments per 12-month period.

note: Membership protections vary by plan; not all benefits are available on all plans. Members should refer to their Member Services Agreement (MSA) or policy for their specific coverage.
